---
description: "Use when creating social media posts for LinkedIn, X/Twitter, or Reddit. Defines platform-specific formatting rules and Unicode character usage."
---

# Social Media Formatting Rules

## LinkedIn & X/Twitter

- Use Unicode Mathematical Bold Sans-Serif for emphasis: 𝗕𝗼𝗹𝗱
- Use Unicode Mathematical Italic Sans-Serif for contrast: 𝘐𝘵𝘢𝘭𝘪𝘤
- Separators: ━━━ (full-width horizontal bar)
- Sub-bullets: ▸ (right-pointing small triangle)
- Emoji anchors sparingly: ⚠️📊🎯
- Wrap copy-paste content between `── START COPY ──` and `── END COPY ──` markers

## X/Twitter Specifics

- Each tweet must be ≤ 280 characters
- Unicode chars count as 1 char on X/Twitter
- Thread format: numbered tweets (1/N) with hook first
- Include standalone single-tweet summary variant

## Reddit

- Standard Markdown ONLY — no Unicode bold/italic
- Use `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `#` headings, `-` bullet lists
- Include TL;DR at the top
- Conversational, anti-promotional tone
- Link to blog naturally at end, not as primary CTA
- Provide subreddit-specific title variants
